steps.text: Clean amber glass jars and petri dishes according to the glassware cleaning procedures listed in Section 8.1.1.1 of this method.
Clean heavy duty aluminum foil by rinsing three times with hexane or toluene.
Store the cleaned aluminum foil in a pre-cleaned glass petri dish or glass jar.
Clean glass sample storage containers according to glassware cleaning procedures listed in Section 8.1.1.1 of this method.
Evaluate alternative carbon-based sorbents prior to their use.
Activate an 18% weight/weight mixture of Carbopack® C and Celite® 545 at 130 °C (266 °F) for a minimum of 6 hours.
Allow the mixture to cool.
Store the mixture in a desiccator.
Prepare spiking standards quantitatively at a convenient concentration (e.g., 10 ng/mL) or use commercial standards if available.
Prepare stock standard solutions in nonane to enable spiking for the Pre-Sampling Adsorbent Standard.
Prepare stock standard solutions in nonane to enable spiking for the Pre-extraction Filter Recovery Standard.
Bake glassware up to 400 °C (752 °F) for a minimum of 2 hours if necessary to remove contaminants.
Allow glassware to cool after baking.
Cover glassware openings with clean glass fitting caps or cleaned aluminum foil.
Rinse glassware immediately before use with acetone and toluene.
Load the adsorbent modules in a clean area.
Fill a module with 20 to 40 g of XAD–2.
Spike modules before the sampling event.
Add the pre-sampling adsorbent standard to the top quarter of the adsorbent bed rather than onto the top or bottom.
Add sufficient spike to result in the final sample theoretical concentrations specified in Tables 23–7, 23–8, and 23–9.
Follow the XAD–2 with cleaned glass wool and tightly cap both ends of the module.
Perform initial calibration following the procedures in Section 10.
Establish a target concentration for the lowest calibration standard based on the intended use of the method.
Perform seven replicate analyses of a calibration sample prepared at proposed lowest calibration concentration.
Calculate the mean and standard deviation for each analyte in the replicates used in Section 9.3.2.
Determine the Half Range for the Prediction Interval of Results (HRPIR) using Equation 23–13.
Calculate the Upper and Lower Limits for the Prediction Interval of Results (PIR) with Equations 23–14 and 23–15.
Confirm the laboratory's ability to measure analyte concentrations down to the lowest calibration point based on the criteria in Section 1.
Pack a column (20 mm ID, 300 mm in length) with approximately 6 to 12 g of basic alumina.
Pre-elute the column with 50 to 100 mL of hexane.
Transfer the concentrated extract from the previous column cleanup to the top of the basic alumina column.
Allow the sample to flow into the column leaving a small quantity of solvent above the top of the bed.
Rinse the extract container with two additional 1-mL rinses of hexane and apply each rinse to the column separately as the previous addition elutes.
Elute the column with 100 mL hexane to remove the interferences.
Elute the PCDD/PCDF from the column with 20 to 40 mL of 50% methylene chloride in hexane.
Concentrate the eluate to approximately 0.5 mL using the nitrogen evaporative concentrator.
Cut both ends from a 10 mL disposable Pasteur pipette to produce a 10 cm column.
Fire-polish both ends and flare both ends if desired.
Insert a glass wool plug at one end and pack the column with 0.55 g of Carbopack®/Celite® to form an adsorbent bed approximately 2 cm long.
Insert a glass wool plug on top of the bed to hold the adsorbent in place.
Pre-elute the column with 5 mL of toluene followed by 2 mL of methylene chloride:methanol:toluene (15:4:1 v/v), 1 mL of methylene chloride:cyclohexane (1:1 v/v), and 5 mL of hexane.
Add the sample extract to the column.
Rinse the sample container twice with 1 mL portions of hexane and apply separately to the column.
Analyze sample (or another aliquot of the sample) using an alternative column that provides elution order to uniquely quantify the target compounds subject to interference on the first GC column.
Document the required resolution when using a confirmation column.
Determine MDLs following the procedures in Section 9.3.7.
Prepare and analyze a spiked sample (spiked at 1 to 5 times the determined MDL) to confirm MDLs.
Confirm that the target compounds meet the qualitative identification criteria in Section 11.4.3.4.
Repeat the MDL determination with a higher spike concentration until criteria are met.
Tune the instrument to meet the required resolving power in Section 10.2.1 for the desired target compound class.
Assess the resolution at three exact m/z’s representing the low-, mid-, and high-m/z range of the masses used to measure the target compound class.
Verify that the exact mass is within 5 ppm of the required value, optionally using peak matching and the chosen PFK (or FC43) reference peak.
Reanalyze the sample.
Repeat the stack or vent sampling.
Verify pre-extraction standard recoveries are between 20 to 130% for PCDD/PCDF and PAH, and between 20 to 145% for PCB.
Reanalyze the sample and/or prepare and analyze the archive sample.
Repeat the stack test.
Verify response of all pre-analysis standard compounds shows a S/N for every selected ion current profile of >= 10.
Reanalyze the sample.
Verify the Upper PIR Limit is <= 150% and the Lower PIR Limit is >= 50%.
Confirm the lowest calibration point at a higher concentration.
Verify the percent relative standard deviation (%RSD) of the concentrations from the replicate analyses is less than 20% for all target analytes.
Repeat demonstration of precision for failed compounds only.
Verify the average % recovery for each target analyte is within 70 to 130%.
Repeat demonstration of accuracy for failed compounds only.
Report all standards used.
Collect a minimum sample volume of 2.5 dscm.
Determine emission rates of the tetra- octa congeners of chlorinated dibenzo-p-dioxins and dibenzofurans (CDDs/).

preconditions.text: If precleaned amber glass jars and petri dishes are not purchased
If precleaned bottles are not purchased

If preparing heavily soiled glassware, remove surface residuals by soaking in hot soapy water, rinsing with hot water, soaking with a non-chromic acid oxidizing cleaning reagent in a strong acid, rinsing with hot water, and repeating cleaning procedures.

For samples with known or anticipated target compound concentration significantly higher or lower than specified, adjust the pre-sampling adsorbent standard concentration to the expected native compound concentration (no less than 10 times the MDL).
For analysis that includes PAH, use spiked modules within 14 days of preparation.
Repeat the IDC if changes are made to analytical parameters not previously validated during the IDC.
The flow rate of eluate does not exceed 0.5 mL/minute (if it does, discard the column).
If target compounds are not sufficiently resolved from other target compounds or interferences in the sample to meet the requirements in Sections 13.2, 13.3, and/or 13.4
If the MDL confirmation criteria are not met
If the sample specific EDLs are less than 50% of the emission standard, the EDLs are acceptable.
If the recovery of standard compounds is below 70%
If the recovery criteria are still not met after reanalysis
If the pre-extraction standard recovery criteria are not met
If the pre-extraction standard recovery criteria are still not met after reanalysis/archive analysis
If the minimum response S/N of >= 10 is not met
If the PIR limit criteria are not met
If the %RSD is not less than 20% for all target analytes
If the average % recovery for each target analyte is not within 70 to 130%
During the trial burn (for new facilities or an interim status facility applying for a permit) or compliance test (for interim status facilities)

constraints.text: Do not use aluminum foil to wrap or contact filter samples due to the possibility of reaction between the sample and the aluminum.
The use of cellulose thimbles for sample extraction in this method is prohibited.
When adjusting the fortification concentrations in the final sample extract, consider variables such as the aliquot of extract used and injection volume of samples and calibration.
Repeated baking of glassware may cause active sites on the glass surface that may irreversibly adsorb target compounds.
Do not spike the modules in the field.
Load the modules in a clean area to avoid contamination.
The same calibration range used during the IDC must be used for the analysis of field samples.
The lowest calibration standard used to establish the initial calibration must not be less than three times the MDL.
The initial calibration must meet performance criteria in Section 13.9.
The laboratory calibration curve must be set at or below the established target concentration level.
The replicate analyses of the lowest calibration concentrations standards must meet the criteria in Sections 13.9 and 13.17.1.
Consider that establishing the lowest calibration concentration too low may cause repeated failure of ongoing QC requirements.
Do not let the head of the basic alumina column go without solvent.
The first 100 mL hexane eluate is not used for subsequent PCDD/PCDF analysis.
Do not let the head of the Carbopack/Celite column go without solvent.
Any modification of this method, beyond those expressly permitted as options, is considered a major modification subject to application and approval of alternative test procedures.
Recommendations are based on the assumption that half of the extract will be archived before cleanup.
Spike levels may be adjusted for different split levels.
You may assume that nondetects are present at zero concentration.
